Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: First compute total resolution time for each case from first event Receive Ticket to last event Close Ticket Timestamps are given Lets calculate durations Case 101 Receive 202403010800 Close 202403011015 Duration 2 hours 15 minutes 135 minutes Case 102 Receive 202403010805 Close 202403020915 25h10m 1510 minutes Case 103 Receive 202403010810 Close 202403010930 1 hour 20 minutes 80 minutes Case 104 Receive 202403010820 Close 202403020830 24h10m 1450 minutes Case 105 Receive 202403010825 Close 202403030930 49h5m 2945 minutes

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: So the average Lets list in minutes 135 1510 80 1450 2945 Clearly cases 102 104 105 are much longer than 101 and 103 101 and 103 are under 25 hours 102 and 104 about 25 hours 105 about 49 hours So cases 102 104 105 are significantly longer But note 104 is also long

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Now we need to identify patterns Look at the activities Case 101 Simple path Receive Triage Assign Level1 Investigate Resolve Close No escalation All steps quick triage 10 min assign 10 min investigate 40 min wait resolve 60 min close 15 min Case 103 Similar Receive 0810 Triage 0815 5 min Assign 0830 15 min Investigate 0845 15 min Resolve 0915 30 min Close 0930 15 min Case 102 Receive 0805 Triage 0830 25 min Assign 0900 30 min Escalate 1130 25 hours later Investigate 1400 25 hours after escalation Resolve next day 0900 19 hours later Close 0915 Case 104 Receive 0820 Triage 0900 40 min Assign 0930 30 min Investigate 1300 35 hours later Resolve next day 0800 19 hours Close 0830 Case 105 Receive 0825 Triage 0835 10 min Assign 0900 25 min Investigate 0910 10 min Escalate 1000 50 min later Investigate 202403021400 28 hours later Resolve 202403030900 Close 0930

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thus patterns Cases with escalations 102 and 105 have longer total times But case 104 also long without escalation Long waiting times between activities specifically between assignment and investigation and between investigation and resolution Also between escalation and next investigation Many gaps span overnight indicating work stops at end of day and resumes next day investigation at 1400 resolve next day 0900 19 hour gap investigate 1300 resolve 0800 case 105 investigation 1400 March 2 resolve 0900

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Potential root causes 1 Escalations introduce significant delays after escalation 25 hour wait Level2 agents not available 2 Overnight gaps investigated late left until next morning resolution not immediate 3 Long idle times between assignment and investigation case 104 35 hours 4 Bottleneck in investigation Level1 agents take time to start 5 Triage and assignment delays minor 6 Multiple investigations case 105 two Investigate initial insufficient

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Recommendations Implement service level agreements SLAs for each step Ensure after investigation resolution performed promptly Address availability of Level2 agents dedicated team faster response Monitor queues assign to available agents Consider automating triage priority system For overnight gaps 247 support model shift handoffs For case 105 double investigation ensure Level1 agents clear guidelines
